name: anandyandawang-plugins
description: Personal Claude Code plugin marketplace. One folder = one plugin.

plugins.name: grug
plugins.description: Talk like grug brain developer. Very clear, very very simple. Fewest words, simple words, short sentences. Brief, high-level — no overexplain. Keeps all technical truth + code exact. Universal: chat, commits, PRs, code comments, reviews.

plugins.name: terse
plugins.description: Hard global cap on output verbosity: at most ~20% of a normal response. Strict, no exceptions. Every medium — chat, commits, PRs, code comments, reviews, logs, docs. Lead with the answer, then stop. Code and technical values stay exact and complete.

plugins.name: codecat
plugins.description: An ASCII-art cat companion that lives in your coding session. It pads in on its own — greets you when a session starts, loafs and does zoomies between turns, celebrates your git commits and pushes, and curls up at session end. Pure art delivered via hooks; never changes how Claude talks. Frequency is tunable.

plugins.name: clear-pr
plugins.description: Write PR titles and descriptions in anandyandawang's house style. Title: a lowercase imperative summary, like a commit subject (no ticket prefix). Body: a single `## What?` that leads with the change then the why. Lead with what + why, link evidence instead of inlining, backtick every code identifier, be honest about tradeoffs, size to the change. Auto-applies when opening a PR; run /pr to draft one for the current branch.

plugins.name: clear-comments
plugins.description: Write code comments in anandyandawang's house style (used across paywithextend repos like astrada-integrator, wex-integrator, and fis-integrator). Comment sparingly — the code says what, a comment says why. Vocabulary is `// TODO(EX-####):` (always with the unblocking condition) and `// Note:` (an intentional, non-obvious decision); no FIXME/HACK/XXX, no `/* */` block comments, no commented-out code, no restating the line below. Backtick every code identifier. KDoc is a mini-spec for public ports and complex services; inline `//` annotates mapping tables with the source system's label; tests get scenario/section banners. Auto-applies when writing code comments; run /comments to audit the comments on the current branch.
